event: Virgin and Child with Infant Saint John in a Garland of Roses
symbols: garland of roses — Mary as the 'Mystical Rose,' beauty and love surrounding the divine mystery
white roses — Mary's purity and virginity
red roses — the blood of Christ's Passion and martyrdom
butterflies and insects — the transience of earthly life and the resurrection of the soul
sculpted cartouche framing the holy figures — the sacred set apart within created beauty
description: At the center of the composition, within an oval sculpted stone cartouche, a woman in a green-blue dress holds an infant while another small child reaches toward them. Surrounding this central medallion is a lavish garland of roses — pink, white, red, and yellow — interspersed with green leaves. Several insects are visible among the flowers: red admiral butterflies, wasps, and ladybirds. The background is deep black, making the flowers and central scene glow with rich color. The botanical rendering is meticulously detailed.
theological_description: This collaboration between Seghers (flowers) and another artist (central figures) represents a distinctive genre of Flemish devotional art: the flower garland surrounding a sacred image. The Virgin Mary holding the Christ child within a wreath of roses carries deep Marian symbolism. In the Litany of Loreto, Mary is invoked as 'Rosa Mystica' — the Mystical Rose. The Song of Solomon declares 'I am a rose of Sharon, a lily of the valleys' (Song of Solomon 2:1), a verse applied to both Christ and Mary in Christian interpretation. The white roses speak of Mary's purity — 'Behold, the virgin shall conceive' (Isaiah 7:14) — while the red roses anticipate the Passion, when Simeon's prophecy would be fulfilled: 'A sword will pierce your own soul too' (Luke 2:35). The infant John the Baptist reaching toward Jesus echoes the Visitation, when John leaped in Elizabeth's womb (Luke 1:41). The insects among the flowers, particularly the butterflies, serve as symbols of resurrection — the caterpillar's transformation into a winged creature paralleling Christ's passage through death to glorified life. The entire composition becomes a visual meditation on the Incarnation surrounded by the beauty of creation, affirming that 'the Word became flesh' (John 1:14) within the garden of God's good world.

scripture_refs.reason: The Word made flesh — the central mystery depicted within the garland of creation's beauty
The Rose of Sharon — applied to Mary and Christ, surrounding the central sacred image
The virgin who conceives — Mary's purity symbolized by the white roses
The sword that will pierce Mary's soul — foreshadowed in the red roses of Passion
The infant John recognizing Jesus — echoed in the infant Baptist reaching toward the Christ child

_meta.title: A garland of roses, with Red Admirals, wasps, ladybirds and other insects, with the Virgin and Child with the Infant Saint John the Baptist in a sculpted cartouche
